preferred_term: upper-threshold spread (q90 minus q50) on the barrier map
replaces_latent: PT scalar summaries; tracks part of kappa1 story
example_sentence: The upper-threshold spread tightens with temperature and co-varies with the dominant correction strength.
The switching map scale and peak current move together with the fitted first correction amplitude.

preferred_term: ridge width on the switching map
example_sentence: Increasing ridge width enters the same geometry family that predicts the deformation ratio.

preferred_term: PT skew of the threshold distribution
example_sentence: PT skew captures much of the systematic trend in the kappa2/kappa1 ratio before the 22–24 K remainder.

preferred_term: symmetric ridge-shape correction vs the PDF backbone
example_sentence: After subtracting the threshold PDF backbone the leading mismatch is a broad symmetric adjustment along the ridge.

preferred_term: secondary width/slope bump on the ridge
example_sentence: The next mismatch looks like a mix of steepening and width changes near the ridge rather than a pure shift.

preferred_term: clock ratio R(T) on the shared grid
example_sentence: The aging clock ratio on the aligned temperature grid follows barrier spread once a map-scale state term is included.

preferred_term: relaxation time A(T) via barrier shape
example_sentence: Relaxation time tracks low-dimensional barrier shape scores without needing decomposition coefficients in the 24A cohort.
Call it a regime-sensitive deformation ratio; note a residual near 22–24 K not captured by PT skew/width alone.
